title: Brevo vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Brevo and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your needs.
intro: Brevo and Buttondown are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Brevo is an all-in-one CRM suite designed for businesses needing multi-channel marketing and transactional email, while Buttondown specializes in newsletters for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown-based writing. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You need multi-channel marketing capabilities including SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat
You want a comprehensive CRM platform with transactional email API
You need to manage large subscriber lists on a free tier (up to 100,000)
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown format
You want the lowest entry price point for paid features
You're a developer or technical writer looking for a simple, focused newsletter tool

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Brevo is better if you want an all-in-one platform with multiple channels and CRM features. Buttondown is better if you're a developer or technical writer who wants a lightweight, markdown-focused tool. Both have strong ratings (Brevo: 4.5/4.3, Buttondown: 4.8/4.7), but serve different use cases.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Brevo or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown has a lower entry price at $9/month compared to Brevo's $25/month for paid plans. However, Brevo's free tier supports up to 100,000 subscribers versus Buttondown's 100, making Brevo more cost-effective if you have a large list before upgrading.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a feature, allowing you to export your subscriber list and content. Most modern email platforms support standard CSV imports, making the migration process straightforward.
